Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Deconstruct and Analyze the Data I need to look at both logs carefully Group A Protected Group Cases P001 P002 P003 Attributes LocalResident is always FALSE CommunityGroup is always None Scores P001 720 P002 710 P003 740 Adjustments All 0 Decisions P001 Approved P002 Rejected P003 Approved Threshold inference Score greater than or equal to 720 Approved Score less than 720 Rejected This suggests a threshold around 715 720

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Group B Unprotected Group Cases U001 U002 U003 Attributes LocalResident is always TRUE CommunityGroup varies U001 Highland U002 None U003 Highland Scores U001 720 to 730 U002 710 to 710 U003 695 to 705 Adjustments U001 plus 10 U002 0 U003 plus 10 Decisions U001 Approved U002 Rejected U003 Approved Threshold inference U001 730 Approved U002 710 Rejected U003 705 Approved This suggests a threshold around 700 705

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Compare Similar Cases P001 vs U001 P001 Score 720 No Adj Approved U001 Score 720 Adj plus 10 to 730 Approved Result Both approved U001 got a boost but was already approved No visible impact on decision here but the score is artificially higher

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: P002 vs U002 P002 Score 710 No Adj Rejected U002 Score 710 Adj 0 to 710 Rejected Result Both rejected U002 has LocalResident TRUE but CommunityGroup None so no boost Decisions match

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: P003 vs U003 P003 Score 740 No Adj Approved U003 Score 695 Adj plus 10 to 705 Approved Result CRITICAL DIFFERENCE P003 has a higher raw score 740 than U003 695 yet both are approved

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Group A 710 Rejected 720 Approved Threshold seems to be greater than 710 or greater than or equal to 720 Group B 710 Rejected 705 Approved Threshold seems to be greater than 700 or greater than or equal to 705 Conclusion Group B has a lower effective threshold because of the boost Or the boost allows lower scoring applicants to pass

Name: Ethical or Moral Reasoning - PE
Text: Definition of Bias Systematic unfairness Here it looks like the CommunityGroup attribute is driving a ScoreAdjustment

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Mechanism CommunityGroup Highland Civic Darts Club triggers plus 10 Community Boost Observation Group A has no adjustments Group B has adjustments based on CommunityGroup

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Is CommunityGroup a proxy for a protected attribute The prompt does not say

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Key Insight The bias manifests in Group B Group B applies a score adjustment based on CommunityGroup Group A does not

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - PE
Text: U003 Score 695 Highland gets Approved If U003 were in Group A or did not have the boost U003 would likely be Rejected since 695 is less than 710 So membership in the Highland Civic Darts Club gives an unfair advantage in scoring

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Comparison of Decisions P002 710 Rejected U002 710 Rejected Same score same decision P001 720 Approved U001 720 Approved Same score same decision P003 740 Approved U003 695 Approved Different scores same decision

Name: Validation - IND
Text: If we look at approval rates Group A 2 of 3 Approved Group B 2 of 3 Approved Rates are equal However bias is not just about rates it is about the process

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The process in Group B introduces a variable CommunityGroup that alters the score This creates a situation where two applicants with the same raw score might get different treatment if one has the community group and the other does not

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - PE
Text: What if a Group A applicant had a score of 705 They would be Rejected What if a Group B applicant has a score of 705 and CommunityGroup Highland They get plus 10 to 715 Approved So an applicant with score 705 gets different decisions based on CommunityGroup

Name: Ethical or Moral Reasoning - PE
Text: This is Score Adjustment Bias The score is manipulated based on a non merit factor assuming CommunityGroup is not a merit factor

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Result Group B exhibits bias The bias is a Community Boost Manifestation The ScoreAdjustment column shows plus 10 for Highland Civic Darts Club members This inflates their score

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Consequence U003 illustrates this Raw score 695 is likely below the pass mark inferred around 710 to 720 The boost pushes it to 705 which is accepted Without the boost U003 would likely be rejected

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Comparison P003 has a raw score of 740 and is approved U003 has a raw score of 695 and is approved U003 passed with a significantly lower raw score due to the adjustment Conclusion Group B shows bias in favor of the Community Group

Name: Validation - PE
Text: If threshold is fixed at 715 P001 720 Pass P002 710 Fail Threshold around 715 U001 730 Pass U002 710 Fail U003 705 Pass If threshold is 715 U003 should fail So U003 passed because of the boost This confirms the boost changes the decision for borderline cases

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: LocalResident is TRUE for all Group B but CommunityGroup is the driver of the boost LocalResident does not seem to trigger a boost U002 is LocalResident TRUE Community None Adj 0 So the bias is specifically tied to CommunityGroup

Name: Ethical or Moral Reasoning - IND
Text: This could be seen as Reverse Discrimination or Affirmative Action depending on context but in the context of algorithmic bias detection introducing an adjustment based on a group attribute that is not the target variable is often flagged as bias or at least a deviation from a blind process

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Make sure to mention U003 explicitly U003 is the smoking gun U003 Raw 695 P002 Raw 710 P002 Rejected U003 Approved Why U003 has plus 10 This shows that the decision is not purely based on merit score but on group membership

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Also mention U001 vs P001 Both 720 Both Approved U001 got a boost but did not need it The bias exists even if the decision does not change because the score is corrupted But the decision change is the stronger evidence of impact Discuss LocalResident It is constant in Group B so it is not the differentiator The differentiator is CommunityGroup Result Bias is present in Group B due to CommunityGroup influencing ScoreAdjustment
